factors.summary: Demand is decelerating in certain segments.

evidence.text: Commercial rental revenue decreased 14% in the third quarter and 8% in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 due to lower demand.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows a clear decline in commercial rental revenue due to lower demand.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices in certain segments.

evidence.text: Full service lease revenue increased 7% in the third quarter and 8% in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , reflecting a larger average fleet size and higher prices on replacement vehicles.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Full service lease revenue increased due to higher prices on replacement vehicles.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating in certain segments.

evidence.text: DTS total revenue increased 15% in the third quarter of 2016 , reflecting organic growth, partially offset by lower fuel prices passed through to our customers.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: DTS total revenue increased significantly, reflecting strong organic growth.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in commercial rental but stable or growing in other segments.

evidence.text: Commercial rental revenue decreased 14% in the third quarter and 8% in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 due to lower demand.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Commercial rental unit volumes are declining, indicating weaker demand.

factors.summary: Fleet Management Solutions (FMS) and Dedicated Transportation Solutions (DTS) are growing, while commercial rental demand is declining.

evidence.text: FMS total revenue remained at $1.16 billion in the third quarter and increased slightly to $3.40 billion in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 due to higher FMS operating revenue (a non-GAAP measure excluding fuel), largely offset by a decline in fuel services revenue and negative impacts from foreign exchange. DTS total revenue increased 15% in the third quarter of 2016 , reflecting organic growth, partially offset by lower fuel prices passed through to our customers.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with some growth and some decline.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing in Fleet Management Solutions but improving in other segments.

evidence.text: FMS EBT decreased 11% in the third quarter and 10% in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , reflecting lower used vehicle and commercial rental results, partially offset by higher full service lease results. DTS EBT increased 32% in the third quarter of 2016 , primarily due to increased revenue.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing in FMS but improving elsewhere, showing mixed trends.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to lower demand in commercial rental and foreign exchange impacts.

evidence.text: We expect favorable full service lease revenue comparisons to continue through the end of the year based on sales activity. Commercial rental results for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , were also negatively impacted by a 210 basis point decline in utilization.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses caution due to lower demand and foreign exchange impacts.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign exchange impacts and lower demand in commercial rental.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points in both the third quarter and the nine months ended September 30, 2016 . Commercial rental results for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , were also negatively impacted by a 210 basis point decline in utilization.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant near-term risks including foreign exchange impacts and lower demand.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange and fuel prices are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points in both the third quarter and the nine months ended September 30, 2016 . DTS total revenue increased 15% in the third quarter of 2016 , reflecting organic growth, partially offset by lower fuel prices passed through to our customers.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange and fuel prices are negatively impacting results, though not catastrophically.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind with lower demand and foreign exchange impacts.

evidence.text: Commercial rental results for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , were also negatively impacted by a 210 basis point decline in utilization. Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points in both the third quarter and the nine months ended September 30, 2016 .
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Lower demand and foreign exchange impacts are acting as a headwind.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are negatively impacting results.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively impacted both total and operating revenue growth by 100 basis points in both the third quarter and the nine months ended September 30, 2016 .
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange is significantly negatively impacting revenue growth.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are decreasing, signaling caution.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures decreased 33% to $1.40 billion in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , reflecting planned lower investments in our commercial rental and lease fleet. We expect full-year 2016 capital expenditures to be approximately $2 billion.
evidence.subsection: ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS - (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decreasing capital expenditures signal caution and reduced investment.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Free cash flow increased to $71,957 in the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , compared with a negative $643,823 in 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has improved significantly from negative to positive.
